title: crotalaria heterotricha
intro: Crotalaria heterotricha, also known as the bushy pea shrub, is a plant species native to tropical Africa and Asia, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and pod-shaped seeds. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, derived from its alkaloid compounds. The health benefits of Crotalaria heterotricha include its potential to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and support the immune system, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in tea, infusion, or decoction form, and is also used as a topical treatment for skin conditions and wounds.
